task: Write a Python function second_largest(nums) returning the second largest distinct value in the list, or None when there are fewer than two distinct values. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The code meets the requirements, but a more efficient way to get the second largest number would be using a single loop over unique numbers
trace: First, I created a function that converts the input list into a set to remove any duplicates. Then, I sorted the resulting set in descending order. If there are less than two unique values (meaning the list has only one or no distinct values), the function returns None. Otherwise, it returns the second largest value by accessing the last but one element of the sorted set. This approach ensures that edge cases where the first and second largest numbers are identical are handled correctly.
